member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AttachmentsController
member.summary: Provides API endpoints for managing file attachments associated with a specific ticket. Supports retrieving, uploading, downloading, and deleting attachments for authorized users.
member.remarks: All actions require the user to be authorized as a manager, reporter, or assigned user for the specified ticket. Endpoints are accessible under the route pattern 'api/tickets/{ticketId}/attachments'. File uploads must use multipart/form-data. Responses include appropriate status codes for authorization and resource existence.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AttachmentsController.#ctor(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.IAuthorizationService,DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.ITicketAttachmentService)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the AttachmentsController class with the specified authorization and ticket attachment services.
member.param: The service used to authorize user actions for attachment-related operations. Cannot be null.
The service responsible for managing ticket attachments. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AttachmentsController.GetAttachments(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves the list of attachments associated with the specified ticket.
member.remarks: Returns status code 200 (OK) with the list of attachments if successful. Returns 403 (Forbidden) if the user is not authorized to access the ticket, or 404 (Not Found) if the ticket does not exist.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket for which to retrieve attachments.
member.returns: An containing a collection of attachment details if the request is authorized and the ticket exists; otherwise, a status code indicating the error.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AttachmentsController.UploadAttachment(System.Int32,DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.AttachementDTOs.UploadFileDTO)
member.summary: Uploads a new attachment file for the specified ticket.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket to which the attachment will be uploaded.
The file data transfer object containing the file to be uploaded.
member.returns: An indicating the result of the upload operation.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AttachmentsController.DownloadAttachment(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Downloads the specified attachment file associated with a ticket.
member.remarks: The user must have appropriate permissions (Manager, Reporter, or AssignedTo) for the specified ticket to download the attachment. Returns a 403 status code if the user is not authorized, or a 404 status code if the attachment does not exist.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket to which the attachment belongs.
The unique identifier of the attachment to download.
member.returns: A file result containing the attachment if found and authorized; otherwise, a 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found response.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AttachmentsController.DeleteAttachment(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Deletes the specified attachment from the given ticket.
member.remarks: The user must have appropriate permissions (Manager, Reporter, or AssignedTo) to delete the attachment. If the user lacks the required authorization, the operation is forbidden.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket from which the attachment will be deleted.
The unique identifier of the attachment to delete.
member.returns: An indicating the result of the delete operation. Returns status code 200 (OK) if the attachment was deleted successfully, 403 (Forbidden) if the user is not authorized, or 404 (Not Found) if the ticket or attachment does not exist.

member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AuthController
member.summary: Authentication controller for handling user login, token refresh, and password reset operations.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AuthController.#ctor(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity.UserManager{DeskFlow.Core.Domain.IdentityEntity.ApplicationUser},DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.IJwtService,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AuthController})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the AuthController class with the specified user manager, JWT service, and logger.
member.param: The user manager used to manage and validate application users.
The service used to generate and validate JSON Web Tokens for authentication.
The logger used to record authentication-related events and errors.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AuthController.login(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.AccountDTOs.LoginDTO)
member.summary: Handles user login
member.param: The login data transfer object containing the user's email and password.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AuthController.refreshToken(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.AccountDTOs.RefreshTokenRequestDTO)
member.summary: refreshes the access token using a valid refresh token.
member.remarks: The client must provide both the expired access token and a valid refresh token. If the refresh token is invalid, expired , or does not match the user, the request will be rejected.
member.param: The refresh token request containing the expired access token and the associated refresh token.
member.returns: Return the new access token and refresh token if successful

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.AuthController.resetPassword(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.AccountDTOs.ResetPasswordRequestDTO)
member.summary: Resets the password for a user identified by the provided email address.
member.param: An object containing the user's email address, current password, and new password.
member.returns: An HTTP 200 OK response if the password is successfully reset

member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.CategoryController
member.summary: Represents an API controller that manages category resources.
member.remarks: Access to this controller is restricted to users with the AdminOnly policy.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.CategoryController.#ctor(DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.ICategoryService)
member.summary: initializes a new instance of the class with the specified category service.
member.param: The service for managing categories.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.CategoryController.GetAllCategories
member.summary: Retrieves all categories.
member.returns: A list of category response DTOs.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.CategoryController.CreateCategory(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.CategoryDTOs.CreateCategoryDTO)
member.summary: Create new category

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.CategoryController.UpdateCategory(System.Int32,DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.CategoryDTOs.UpdateCategoryDTO)
member.summary: Update category by id
member.param: Category Id
Category data to update

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.CategoryController.DeleteCategory(System.Int32)
member.summary: Delete category by id
member.param: Category Id

member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.DepartmentController
member.summary: Represents an API controller that manages department resources.
member.remarks: Access to this controller is restricted to users with the AdminOnly policy

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.DepartmentController.#ctor(DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.IDepartmentService)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified department service.
member.param: The service for managing departments.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.DepartmentController.GetAllDepartments
member.summary: Retrive all the departments
member.returns: list of departments

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.DepartmentController.DeleteDepartment(System.Int32)
member.summary: Deletes the department with Id.
member.param: The Id of the department to delete.
member.returns: An HTTP 204 No Content response if the department was successfully deleted.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.DepartmentController.AddDepartment(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.DepartmentDTO.DepartmentCreateDTO)
member.summary: Creates a new department with the specified name.
member.param: The DTO containing the details of the department to create. Cannot be null.

member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.SLAController
member.summary: Provides API endpoints for managing Service Level Agreement (SLA) resources.
member.remarks: Access to these endpoints is restricted to users with the AdminOnly policy.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.SLAController.#ctor(DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.ISLAService)
member.summary: initializes a new instance of the class with the specified SLA service.
member.param: The service for managing SLAs.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.SLAController.CreateSLA(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.SLADTOs.SLACreateDTO)
member.summary: Create new SLA

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.SLAController.GetAllSLA
member.summary: Retrieves all service level agreements (SLAs).

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.SLAController.GetSLAById(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves a specific service level agreement (SLA) by its Id.
member.param: SLA Id

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.SLAController.UpdateSLA(System.Int32,DeskFlow.Core.Domain.Entity.SLASetting)
member.summary: Updates the specified SLA setting with new values.
member.param: The unique identifier of the SLA setting to update. Must match the Id property of the provided SLA setting.
The updated SLA setting values. The Id property must match the id parameter.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.SLAController.DeleteSLA(System.Int32)
member.summary: Delete SLA by its Id
member.param: SLA Id

member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketCommentController
member.summary: for managing comments related to tickets.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketCommentController.#ctor(DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.ITicketCommentsService,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.IAuthorizationService)
member.summary: intializes a new instance of the class with the specified ticket comments service and authorization service.
member.param: The service for managing ticket comments.
The service for handling authorization checks.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketCommentController.AddComment(System.Int32,DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.CommentDTOs.CommentCreateDTO)
member.summary: Adds a new comment to the specified ticket.
member.remarks: Admin, Depatment Manger, Assigned technician and Reported User only can comment
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket to which the comment will be added.
The data for the comment to add, including content and associated ticket information. Must not be null, and the TicketId property must match the ticketId parameter.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketCommentController.GetComments(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrive all comments related to a specific ticket by providing the ticket's unique identifier.
member.remarks: Admin, Depatment Manger, Assigned technician and Reported User only can access the comments of the ticket

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketCommentController.DeleteComment(System.Int32)
member.summary: Delete a comment from a ticket by providing the ticket's unique identifier and the comment's unique identifier.
member.remarks: Only the comment's author or an admin can delete a comment.

member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController
member.summary: Controller for managing support tickets, providing endpoints for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting tickets, as well as managing ticket status, priority, and assignment. Access to these endpoints is restricted based on user roles and authorization policies.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.#ctor(DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.ITicketService,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.IAuthorizationService)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the TicketsController class with the specified ticket and authorization services.
member.param: The service used to manage and retrieve ticket data.
The service used to perform authorization checks for ticket-related operations.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.CreateTicket(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.TicketDTOs.CreateTicketDTO)
member.summary: open new ticket.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.GetTicketById(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrive ticket by Id.
member.remarks: Only the reporter, assigned technician, manager of the department or admin can access the ticket details.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.GetAllTickets(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.TicketDTOs.TicketQueryParams)
member.summary: Retrives all tickets with pagination and optional filtering by status, priority, department, category, reporter or assigned technician.
member.param: The query parameters for filtering and pagination.
member.returns: A list of tickets matching the specified criteria.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.UpdateTicket(System.Int32,DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.TicketDTOs.UpdateTicketDTO)
member.summary: Udpate all ticket details except status, assigned technician and priority.
member.remarks: Only the reporter, assigned technician, manager of the department or admin can update the ticket details.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket to update. Cannot be null or empty.
An object containing the updated ticket details. All fields are required.
member.returns: A 204 No Content response if the ticket is updated successfully; otherwise, a 400 Bad Request response containing error details.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.UpdateTicketStatus(System.Int32,DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.TicketDTOs.TicketUpdateStatusDTO)
member.summary: Updates the status of the specified ticket to a new value.
member.remarks: This operation requires the caller to have appropriate permissions to modify the ticket.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket to update.
An object containing the new status value and an optional comment.
member.returns: A result indicating the outcome of the operation. Returns 204 No Content if the update is successful, or 400 Bad Request if the new status value is invalid.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.AssignTechnician(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Assigns a technician to the specified support ticket.
member.remarks: This action requires the caller to have the ManagerOrTechnician authorization policy.
member.param: The unique identifier of the support ticket to which the technician will be assigned.
The unique identifier of the technician to assign to the ticket. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A result indicating the outcome of the operation. Returns a 204 No Content response if the assignment is successful.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.UpdateTicketPriority(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Updates the priority of the specified ticket.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket to update.
The new priority value to assign to the ticket. Must be an integer between 1 and 3, inclusive.
member.returns: A result indicating the outcome of the operation. Returns 204 No Content if the update is successful, or 400 Bad Request if the new priority value is invalid.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.DeleteTicket(System.Int32)
member.summary: delete ticket of specidied Id
member.remarks: Admin only can delete tickets.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket to delete.
member.returns: A result indicating the outcome of the operation. Returns 204 No Content if the deletion is successful, or 400 Bad Request if the ticket cannot be deleted.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.TicketsController.GetTicketHistory(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves the audit history for a specified ticket.
member.remarks: Returns a 404 response if the ticket does not exist, or a 401 response if the caller does not have permission to view the ticket's history.
member.param: The unique identifier of the ticket for which to retrieve the audit history. Must be a valid ticket ID.
member.returns: An containing a collection of objects representing the ticket's audit history if found and authorized; otherwise, an error response indicating the reason for failure.

member.name: T:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController
member.summary: Controller for managing user accounts, including registration, updating, deletion, and retrieval of user details. Access to these endpoints is restricted based on user roles and policies.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController.#ctor(DeskFlow.Core.ServicesContracts.IUserService,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController},Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization.IAuthorizationService)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the UsersController class with the specified user service, logger, and authorization service.
member.param: The service used to manage user-related operations. Cannot be null.
The logger used to record diagnostic and operational information for the controller. Cannot be null.
The authorization service used to enforce policies and requirements. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController.register(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.UsersDTOs.RegisterUserDTO)
member.summary: Registers a new user account with the specified registration details. Accessible only to users in the Admin role.
member.remarks: This action requires authentication and is restricted to users with the Admin role.
member.param: An object containing the registration information for the new user, including email, full name, department, phone number, and password. All fields are required.
member.returns: A 201 Created result if the user is successfully registered; otherwise, a 400 Bad Request result containing validation errors or identity errors.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController.UpdateUser(System.String,DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.UsersDTOs.UpdateUserDTO)
member.summary: Update user deatails
member.remarks: Accessible only for Admin users.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController.DeleteUser(System.String)
member.summary: Deletes the user with the specified identifier.
member.remarks: Accissable only for admin role.
member.param: The unique identifier of the user to delete. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A 204 No Content response if the user is deleted successfully; otherwise, a 400 Bad Request response containing error details.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController.GetUserById(System.String)
member.summary: Get user deatails by id.
member.remarks: Accessible for Admin, Manager and the user himself. Managers can only access users in their department.
member.param: The unique identifier of the user to retrieve. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A 200 OK response containing the user details if found; otherwise, a 404 Not Found response.

member.name: M:DeskFlow.WebAPI.Controllers.UsersController.GetAllUsers(DeskFlow.Core.DTOs.UsersDTOs.UsersQueryParameters)
member.summary: Retrives a list of users based on the specified query parameter.
member.remarks: Accessible for Admin and Manager roles. Managers can only access users in their department.
member.param: The query parameters for filtering and pagination.
member.returns: A 200 OK response containing the list of users if found; otherwise, a 404 Not Found response.

member.name: T:Program
member.summary: Auto-generated public partial Program class for top-level statement apps.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Type)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a type. Example: T:Namespace.Outer+Inner`1 becomes T:Namespace.Outer.Inner`1

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Reflection.PropertyInfo)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a property. Example: P:Namespace.ContainingType.PropertyName or for an indexer P:Namespace.ContainingType.Item(System.Int32)

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Type,System.String)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a property given its container type and property name. Example: P:Namespace.ContainingType.PropertyName

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.CreateDocumentationId(System.Reflection.MethodInfo)
member.summary: Generates a documentation comment ID for a method (or constructor). For example: M:Namespace.ContainingType.MethodName(ParamType1,ParamType2)~ReturnType M:Namespace.ContainingType.#ctor(ParamType)

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.GetTypeDocId(System.Type,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generates a documentation ID string for a type. This method handles nested types (replacing '+' with '.'), generic types, arrays, pointers, by-ref types, and generic parameters. The flag controls whether constructed generic type arguments are emitted, while controls whether the generic arity marker (e.g. "`1") is appended.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi.Generated.DocumentationCommentIdHelper.NormalizeDocId(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a documentation comment ID to match the compiler-style format. Strips the return type suffix for ordinary methods but retains it for conversion operators.
member.param: The documentation comment ID to normalize.
member.returns: The normalized documentation comment ID.
